methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Salient Motion is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Vishaal Mali that has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private companies and one public entity operating in Robotics & Manufacturing, including peers such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient clustering within narrow stage and valuation-tier filters—while seven same-sector peers exist, fewer than three met the criteria of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparability. The analysis therefore relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $12M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $24M.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $20M to $28M, with a median of $24M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty given the limited peer comparability. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this company's specific growth trajectory and market conditions, and the absence of direct trading or transaction multiples in the narrow peer set. A broader peer sample or additional transaction data would strengthen conviction in this range.
